concept.conceptName: Lactate dehydrogenase | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge

en.name: Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) is a cytoplasmic enzyme present in most tissues; its release into the blood follows cellular damage. The serum or plasma activity is measured as a non-specific marker of tissue injury (haemolysis, liver, muscle, malignancy) [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as an enzymatic activity in U/L (international units per litre) [2]. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on one LOINC Hierarchy concept (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with its descendants pulled in. The set resolves to three LOINC "Lab Test" concepts in serum or plasma. ### Serum or plasma (default specimen) - *3016436 / 2532-0 — Lactate dehydrogenase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: generic LOINC code, method-agnostic. Most widely used. - *3022250 / 14804-9 — Lactate dehydrogenase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Lactate to pyruvate reaction (LOINC)*: same measurand, with the assay direction explicit. - *3005225 / 14805-6 — Lactate dehydrogenase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Pyruvate to lactate reaction (LOINC)*: same measurand, with the assay direction explicit. The two reaction directions yield different absolute values (lactate→pyruvate is conventionally ~2× higher than pyruvate→lactate); the LOINC code preserves this method information. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`U/L`** (concept 8645 — unit per litre). Source systems may report `U/L` or `IU/L`; the two are numerically equal. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Red Blood Cells specimens**: intra-erythrocytic LDH, specialised research measurement. - **Urine, CSF, body fluid and other specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **LDH isoenzymes** (LDH-1 through LDH-5): different measurands, captured by dedicated concept sets when needed. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "LDH", "lactate dehydrogenase", "lactic acid dehydrogenase" without further specification: `3016436 / 2532-0 — Lactate dehydrogenase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source explicitly mentions the assay direction**: map to the corresponding `14804-9` (lactate-to-pyruvate) or `14805-6` (pyruvate-to-lactate) code. The two are **not numerically equivalent** — preserve the source method. - **Do not confuse with**: LDH isoenzymes (LDH-1 to LDH-5), lactate (the metabolite, a different concept set), pyruvate. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2532-0 — Lactate dehydrogenase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/2532-0/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
